articles.title: Rochdale Hornets Defeated by Wigan in Lancashire Cup
articles.excerpt: In the Lancashire Cup 1946/47, Rochdale Hornets lost to Wigan with a score of 32 to 5 in the second leg of the first round.
articles.content_html:
In the Lancashire Cup 1946/47, Rochdale Hornets faced Wigan in the second leg of the first round.

The match concluded with Rochdale Hornets scoring 5 points, while Wigan secured a decisive victory with 32 points.

This result marks a significant moment in the history of both clubs, reflecting the competitive nature of the tournament during that season.

The match took place on July 17, 2026, and is part of the ongoing documentation of rugby league history.

articles.title: Rochdale AFC Suffers 2-0 Defeat in Pre-Season Friendly Against FC United
articles.excerpt: Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson reflects on the team's recent outing against FC United, highlighting key moments and future aspirations.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 14 July 2026 at 21:52. 7 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson reflects on the team's recent outing against FC United, highlighting key moments and future aspirations.

Latest update

Rochdale AFC's first pre-season outing ended in a 2-0 defeat at FC United of Manchester on Tuesday evening. The match marked the beginning of the team's preparations ahead of the upcoming season.

Despite a spirited performance, Rochdale struggled to find the back of the net, while FC United capitalised on their chances to secure the win.

The defeat serves as an early reminder of the challenges ahead as the team looks to build momentum in the weeks leading up to the new season.

Fans will be hoping for improved results in the upcoming fixtures as the squad continues to develop and refine their tactics.

Earlier developments

Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson has spoken about the team's recent outing against FC United. In his comments, he reflected on the performance and the lessons learned from the match.

Watson noted the importance of maintaining focus and discipline throughout the game. He emphasised that every match provides an opportunity for growth and improvement.

Looking ahead, Watson expressed optimism about the team's potential and the upcoming fixtures. He believes that with continued effort, the squad can achieve its goals this season.

The manager's insights come as Rochdale AFC prepares for its next challenge, aiming to build on the experiences gained from the FC United match.

Update timeline

articles.title: Pupils quiz Paul Waugh about Parliament during Littleborough school visit
articles.excerpt: Pupils at St Mary’s RC Primary School in Littleborough questioned Paul Waugh about Parliament during a special assembly ahead of England’s match.
articles.summary: Paul Waugh visited St Mary’s RC Primary School in Littleborough, where pupils wearing England shirts took part in an assembly about Parliament and the history of democracy.
articles.content_html:
Pupils at St Mary’s RC Primary School in Littleborough welcomed Paul Waugh for a special assembly about Parliament and democracy.

Children asked questions about how Parliament works and discussed England’s and Rochdale’s history of democratic participation.

The visit took place ahead of England’s match on Saturday evening, with many pupils attending the assembly in England football shirts and other red-and-white clothing.

Questions about Parliament

Waugh thanked headteacher Mrs Ruane, school staff and pupils for the welcome and for the questions raised during the session.

Photographs shared following the visit showed pupils taking part in a group discussion before listening to a presentation titled All About Parliament.

The session gave children an opportunity to learn more about political representation, democratic institutions and the role Parliament plays in national life.

England colours at school

The school community also marked the occasion by wearing England tops ahead of the evening’s football fixture.

Waugh said it was good to see the pupils showing their support and expressed hope that they had learned more about the country’s democratic history during the visit.

This report is based on a public Facebook update and photographs published by Paul Waugh following his visit to St Mary’s RC Primary School in Littleborough.
articles.event_location: St Mary’s RC Primary School, Littleborough
articles.right_to_reply: Paul Waugh, St Mary’s RC Primary School and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

The video has gained traction on the platform, featuring hashtags such as #rochdale, #foryou, and #bbq, which are popular among local users. The light-hearted nature of the post has resonated with the community, prompting comments and interactions from viewers.

articles.title: Geoff Hurst Reflects on His Iconic 1966 World Cup Hat-Trick
articles.excerpt: As England prepares to face Norway, Geoff Hurst recalls his historic hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final, marking 60 years since the memorable match.
articles.content_html:
Geoff Hurst, the first player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final, reflected on his iconic performance during England's 4-2 victory over West Germany in 1966. As England prepares to face Norway later today, Hurst shared his memories of that historic match, which took place at Wembley Stadium.

In the final minute of extra time, Hurst received a long ball from Bobby Moore while unmarked. Exhausted, he decided to strike the ball as hard as he could. "And of course, I mis-hit it, and it flew in," Hurst recalled in a 2018 interview with BBC Radio 4, reminiscing about the final goal of his hat-trick.

Born in 1941 in Ashton-under-Lyne, Hurst grew up in a footballing family. His father, Charlie Hurst, was also a professional footballer, having played for clubs including Oldham Athletic, Rochdale, and Mossley before moving south to join Chelmsford City.

As the anniversary of his remarkable achievement approaches, Hurst's contributions to English football remain celebrated, highlighting the significance of his performance in shaping the nation's sporting history.

articles.title: Junior parkrun for children to take place at Queen’s Park
articles.excerpt: A junior parkrun event for children aged 4 to 14 will be held at Queen’s Park in Rochdale this Sunday at 9am, featuring a 2k run, walk, skip or jump.
articles.content_html:
A junior parkrun event will take place at Queen’s Park in Rochdale this Sunday, July 12, at 9am. The event is open to children aged 4 to 14 and will feature a 2k run, walk, skip or jump.

Participants are encouraged to enjoy the outdoors and engage in physical activity in a supportive environment. The event aims to promote fitness and fun among young people.

Parents and guardians are welcome to accompany their children during the event. Registration is not required, and all abilities are welcome to join in.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to encourage healthy lifestyles among children in the community. For more information, visit the parkrun website.

articles.title: Rochdale AFC Prepares for FC United Match
articles.excerpt: Thousands of supporters are gathering for England’s World Cup quarter-final against Norway, with Rochdale venues also advertising screenings of tonight’s 10pm match.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 11 July 2026 at 21:03. 6 source updates have been combined into this article.

Thousands of supporters are gathering for England’s World Cup quarter-final against Norway, with Rochdale venues also advertising screenings of tonight’s 10pm match.

Latest update

Thousands of supporters are gathering for England’s World Cup quarter-final against Norway, with Rochdale venues also advertising screenings of tonight’s 10pm match.

Football supporters have formed a huge queue outside Depot Mayfield in Manchester ahead of England’s World Cup quarter-final against Norway tonight.

Images shared shortly before the match showed fans lining the surrounding streets as thousands prepared to watch the game at the city-centre venue.

Norway and England are due to kick off at 10pm UK time at the Hard Rock Stadium in Miami.

Earlier developments

England reached the last eight after defeating Mexico 3-2, while Norway progressed with a 2-1 victory over Brazil.

Google-indexed event listings checked by Rochdale Daily at the time of publication identified the following Rochdale town-centre screenings:

Supporters should contact the venue or check its booking page before travelling, as entry arrangements, remaining capacity and ticket availability may change.

Licensing hours have been extended for the match, allowing participating pubs in England and Wales to remain open until 2am if the game is delayed or continues into extra time and penalties.

The late kick-off is a result of the match being played in Miami, where it begins at 5pm local time.

England will face either Argentina or Switzerland in the semi-final if Thomas Tuchel’s side defeats Norway.

This report is based on a Manchester Evening News Facebook update supplied to Rochdale Daily, live national match reporting and Google-indexed event listings checked at approximately 9.03pm on Saturday 11 July 2026. Inclusion does not guarantee admission or remaining capacity.

Sir Geoff Hurst, the first player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final, reflected on his historic achievement as England prepares to face Norway later today. In the final minute of extra time during the 1966 World Cup final against West Germany, Hurst received a long ball from Bobby Moore and, despite feeling exhausted, struck the ball with all his might. "And of course, I mis-hit it, and it flew in," he recounted in a 2018 interview with BBC Radio 4, recalling the final goal of his hat-trick in England's 4-2 victory at Wembley.

articles.title: Indoor 5 A Side Football Sessions Announced at Whitworth Swimming Baths
articles.excerpt: New Back Care Yoga sessions are set to begin at Whitworth Swimming Baths, aimed at improving posture and alleviating back pain for participants.
articles.content_html:
A series of Back Care Yoga sessions will commence at Whitworth Swimming Baths, designed to assist individuals in improving their posture and managing back pain. The initiative aims to provide a supportive environment for participants to engage in gentle yoga practices that focus on strengthening the back and enhancing flexibility.

The sessions will be led by qualified instructors who specialise in back care and yoga therapy. Each class will incorporate techniques that promote relaxation and mindfulness, alongside physical exercises tailored to address common back issues. Participants of all skill levels are welcome, making it an inclusive opportunity for those seeking relief from discomfort or wishing to enhance their overall well-being.

Classes are scheduled to run weekly, with the first session set to take place on . Attendees are encouraged to wear comfortable clothing and bring their own yoga mats, although mats will be available for those who do not have one. The sessions are expected to last approximately one hour, allowing ample time for instruction and practice.

Local health professionals have endorsed the programme, recognising the benefits of yoga in managing back pain and improving posture. They highlight that regular participation in such activities can lead to significant improvements in physical health and quality of life.

The Back Care Yoga sessions at Whitworth Swimming Baths represent a valuable addition to the community's health and wellness offerings. They provide an accessible way for residents to engage in physical activity while focusing on specific health concerns related to the back.

For more information about the sessions, including how to register, interested individuals can contact Whitworth Swimming Baths directly or visit their website. The initiative is part of a broader effort to promote health and fitness within the local community.
